title: Costume Contest

jokes.text: Character trying on someone else's costume without permission
jokes.setup: Character asking about a costume being 'badass'
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it's not even theirs
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe moment but lacks character specificity or strong setup

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: This costume is badass.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Whose is it?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's not even mine.

jokes.text: Character not noticing it wasn't his hot coffee
jokes.setup: Character being oblivious about basic things
jokes.punchline: Question about limits to what he won't notice
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven observational humor with escalating absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I have exquisite taste in all things. I can tell the difference between a 1987 and 1988 Rolex at fifty paces.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight, I replaced your stapler with a identical one.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: What? No you didn't.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I did. You've been using it all day.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: That's... that's preposterous.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So my question is, how much would I have to change before you'd notice? Like, what if I replaced you with a robot?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I'd notice immediately.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Would you though?

jokes.text: Handbag as answer to seven-letter crossword clue
jokes.punchline: Simple, obvious answer provided
jokes.explanation: Minor workplace humor, lacks punch

jokes.text: Branch on planet Jupiter is up 8,000% in sales
jokes.punchline: Completely impossible location for office branch
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor delivered deadpan in business context

jokes.text: Stanley immediately leaving at 5:00 despite meeting
jokes.punchline: Stanley's strict adherence to leaving time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley character moment - his priorities are clear and unwavering

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: I don't care if we're in the middle of a nuclear explosion. At 5 PM, I leave. And nothing—not Michael, not Dwight, not a direct order from corporate—is going to stop me.

jokes.text: 'Love you' said to Stanley as he leaves
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly affectionate goodbye to unresponsive Stanley
jokes.explanation: Nice awkward beat but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Stanley, wait! Where are you going?

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: I'm leaving.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Stanley, come on. We're a family here. A work family.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I love you, man.

jokes.text: Kevin saying he looks like Michael Moore as his costume statement
jokes.setup: Oscar asking about Kevin's costume statement
jokes.punchline: Kevin's simple, literal observation about resembling Michael Moore
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment - his straightforward thinking contrasts with Oscar's pretension

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: What are you supposed to be?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I'm Michael Moore.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Why?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Because I look like him.

jokes.text: Erin answering phone 'How can I haunt you today?'
jokes.punchline: Halloween pun on standard phone greeting
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Erin's enthusiastic character perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Hello, you've reached Dunder Mifflin. How can I help you? Oh, and happy Halloween! If you need to leave a message, press 1. If you're a ghost, press 2. If you're a werewolf, press 3. If you're just regular scary, press 4.

jokes.text: MacGruber countdown interrupting normal conversation
jokes.punchline: Sudden MacGruber bomb defusal routine interruption
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference bit that feels somewhat forced

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: So what are your Halloween plans?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: I don't know, maybe something low-key this year.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: Wait, is that a bomb?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: MacGruber! We need MacGruber!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown
dialogue.line: *frantically cuts wires* Okay, okay, I got this. Just stay calm everyone!

jokes.text: Coupon book worth $15,000 in savings requiring spending $200,000
jokes.punchline: Mathematical reality of coupon book economics
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about marketing deception that many viewers can relate to

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: This is a $500 coupon book. You can save up to $500!

dialogue.speaker: Dwight Schrute
dialogue.line: That's not how coupons work.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: Yes it is. It says right here: up to $500.

dialogue.speaker: Jim Halpert
dialogue.line: Michael, you have to actually use the coupons to save money.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: I know that. But the potential is there. Up to $500.

jokes.text: If I was the real Scranton strangler, you'd be so strangled by now
jokes.punchline: Overly helpful explanation of how effective he'd be
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good character voice - the earnestness makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I am the Scranton Strangler. And let me tell you, I would be very effective. I have extensive knowledge of pressure points, I'm extremely disciplined, and I have access to industrial-grade materials.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight, why are you explaining this?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Because you asked how effective I'd be. I'm being thorough.

jokes.text: To my chickens, I'm the Scranton strangler
jokes.punchline: Creed's matter-of-fact admission about his chickens
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed line - dark, unexpected, economical, and completely in character. Peak Office writing.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: I have chickens. I'm not gonna lie to you, I don't know how many.

jokes.text: Dwight insulting Angela's Olive Oyl costume as his mother
jokes.punchline: Dwight comparing her unfavorably to his mother
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics - Dwight's specific way of insulting fits perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Angela, you look like Olive Oyl, but not in a good way. My mother looked like Olive Oyl, and she was a beautiful woman. You look like Olive Oyl if she had given up on life.

jokes.text: Jim refusing to put on Popeye costume despite Pam's Olive Oyl
jokes.punchline: Jim's resistance to participating
jokes.explanation: Relatable relationship dynamic but not particularly strong as comedy

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: We could be Mario and Luigi for Halloween!

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Pam, no.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Come on, it would be so cute!

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I'm not wearing overalls and a fake mustache.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: You wore a tuxedo to the Dundies.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That was different. That was dignified.

jokes.text: Jim's talking head about being too old for costumes even as a kid
jokes.punchline: Claiming he was too old for costumes even as a child
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Jim's self-aware smugness comes through clearly

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I'm not really a costume guy. I never was, even as a kid. I was like the one six-year-old who was like, 'No, I'm not doing this.'

jokes.text: MacGruber explaining sales policies with bomb countdown
jokes.setup: Serious business meeting about sales policies
jokes.punchline: Delivering information through MacGruber bomb defusal bit
jokes.explanation: Forced pop culture bit that doesn't integrate well with story

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: Okay everyone, we need to talk about our sales policies. This is serious.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: There's a bomb. And the only way to defuse it is to listen to me read these new guidelines.

dialogue.speaker: Jim Halpert
dialogue.line: Wait, what? There's an actual bomb?

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: No, no. It's a metaphorical bomb. But you need to pay attention like it's real.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight Schrute
dialogue.line: I can defuse a real bomb.

dialogue.speaker: Michael Scott
dialogue.line: That's great, Dwight. But right now we're doing this my way. MacGruber style.

jokes.text: Why don't witches wear panties? To grip the broom
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo punchline
jokes.explanation: Generic dirty joke that could be told anywhere - lacks character specificity

jokes.text: Where does Dracula like to water ski? Lake Erie
jokes.punchline: Eerie/Erie pun
jokes.explanation: Basic pun joke - predictable and not character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: What's the difference between something eerie and the Erie Canal?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I don't know, what?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: One is a spooky feeling and the other is a waterway. The spelling is slightly different — eerie versus Erie. It's a homophone-based pun.

jokes.text: Has anyone started calling you Gabe-wad yet?
jokes.punchline: Immediate mocking of his name
jokes.explanation: Good character undermining - highlights Gabe's lack of respect

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: I am asserting my authority as the head of this office.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Did you say 'Gabe'? That sounds like the noise a baby makes when it's choking.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Gabe. Gabe. Gabe. It's like a sound effect for a broken door hinge.

jokes.text: Darryl hiding ream of paper in shirt thinking he might get hit
jokes.setup: Tense conversation about credit for ideas
jokes.punchline: Darryl's preparation for physical confrontation
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy with character insight - Darryl knows Michael well enough to prepare

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm about to go full Thundergun Express on you right now.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Darryl, come on—

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I've been working on my core. I've been doing Pilates. I am ready to throw down.

jokes.text: Michael's talking head about bringing racists to lunch with Darryl
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate and offensive 'solution' to racism
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael Scott cringe - well-intentioned but completely tone-deaf

jokes.text: Oscar explaining coupon book math while everyone ignores him
jokes.punchline: Everyone's complete disinterest in facts
jokes.explanation: Good Oscar moment - his rationality goes unappreciated as usual

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Everyone's complete disinterest in facts

jokes.text: Public School is the name of Danny's bar
jokes.setup: Danny saying he owns a bar
jokes.punchline: Bar name sounds like location description
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that creates brief confusion

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: Yeah, I own a bar.

dialogue.speaker: Other Character
dialogue.line: What's it called?

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: The Local.

jokes.text: Pam choosing whoever smells most like her father
jokes.setup: Discussion about Pam choosing between Jim and Danny
jokes.punchline: Freudian explanation involving father's scent
jokes.explanation: Weird psychological humor but doesn't feel specifically tied to characters

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's a Freudian thing. She's attracted to the scent of her father.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight, that doesn't even make sense.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: She's subconsciously looking for a paternal figure.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: My dad smells like Old Spice and regret.

jokes.text: Jim and Pam were Romeo and Juliet except Juliet didn't have a great time
jokes.setup: Everyone treating Jim/Pam/Danny as dramatic love triangle
jokes.punchline: Deconstructing the romantic narrative with reality
jokes.explanation: Sharp writing that punctures romantic comedy tropes with truth

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. There's no love triangle here.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm married to Jim.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: And Danny's our friend.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: A friend who just happens to be attractive.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: But that doesn't mean anything's going on.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Right. We're not in a TV show.

jokes.text: I'm like an elephant in that way. You know what eleph... this sucks for Jim
jokes.setup: Michael claiming to have elephant memory
jokes.punchline: Self-interrupting when he can't remember elephant facts
jokes.explanation: Good Michael moment - his self-awareness kicks in at the wrong time

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I have an elephant memory. I never forget anything.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Like, did you know that elephants are afraid of—wait, what was I saying?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Right, elephants. They can remember things for like... how long do they live? A hundred years?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Actually, I have no idea. I totally made that up.

jokes.text: Michael trying to get shy Darryl to come out and embrace being a superstar
jokes.punchline: Darryl's obvious discomfort with attention
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic but not exceptional comedy

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Darryl, I want everyone to see how valuable you are to this company. You're the heart of Dunder Mifflin!

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Michael, I appreciate that, but you don't have to—

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I'm putting you on a pedestal. Literally. Everyone gather around!

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Why is there a pedestal in the middle of the office?

jokes.text: Michael being peeved that Darryl went over his head to go behind his back
jokes.punchline: Confusing directional metaphors
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that shows character confusion about office politics

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Todd, you can't keep going around me on decisions. That's a direct violation of the chain of command.

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.line: But Michael, I thought you said to go around you on the budget cuts?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, that was... that was different. That was horizontally around me, not vertically around me.

dialogue.speaker: Todd
dialogue.line: I'm not following.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Exactly.

jokes.text: Andy and Kevin telling Danny that Jim and Pam begged them not to go
jokes.setup: Danny inviting people to his party
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing creating more drama
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven awkwardness but not standout

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: Hey guys, I'm throwing a party this weekend. You should come!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh cool, man. What kind of party?

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: Well, it's kind of a celebration. My therapist says I'm finally dealing with my abandonment issues from my childhood, so I wanted to mark the occasion.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Uh... that's... nice?

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: Yeah, and my ex-wife is gonna be there too. We're co-parenting now, which is great because it really forces me to confront my fear of intimacy.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Maybe keep some of that private, Danny.

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: Oh, and I should mention — I've been on antidepressants for six months, and I just switched to a new one, so I might be a little emotional.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I'm suddenly very busy that weekend.

jokes.text: Kevin and Gabe discussing extremes of human physique
jokes.setup: Michael paranoid about people going over his head
jokes.punchline: They're actually discussing their physical differences
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on Michael's paranoia

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Why are you guys huddled in the corner whispering about me?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: We're not talking about you.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: We were just discussing how Kevin is so much taller than me.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh.

jokes.text: Kevin teaching Gabe Lady Gaga robot power-down move
jokes.punchline: Kevin just helping with costume moves
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy with character dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Kevin, why are you and Gabe always huddled together in the corner?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: We're not huddled. Gabe's helping me with my costume moves for the Halloween party.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Costume moves? What kind of costume moves?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: You know, like practicing my zombie walk. Gabe said I look too stiff.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: His posture is atrocious.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh. So you're just... helping him with his zombie walk.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Yeah. Why, what did you think we were doing?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Nothing. I just thought maybe you were plotting against me or something.

jokes.text: Danny not remembering if it was two or three dates with Pam
jokes.setup: Trying to minimize their dating history
jokes.punchline: Danny's casual attitude reveals how unmemorable it was
jokes.explanation: Good awkward relationship humor

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So how many people have you dated?

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: I don't know, maybe like... five? Six?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: You don't know?

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: They kind of blur together, you know? It wasn't a big deal.

jokes.text: You can't handle the truth! / Well, that does not sound like me
jokes.setup: Pam explaining Danny never called back
jokes.punchline: Danny's complete lack of self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good character blindness to own behavior

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Danny never called me back.

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: I've been really busy.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It's been three months.

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: Yeah, like I said. Busy.

jokes.text: Suck it
jokes.punchline: Michael's childish response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Michael moment - petty and childish

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not childish. You're childish.

jokes.text: Oscar as rational consumer costume
jokes.punchline: Oscar's meta-commentary costume choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar costume - intellectually superior and judgmental

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: I'm going as a man who works at a mid-size paper company in Scranton, Pennsylvania.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: It's a very specific costume.

jokes.text: Michael's Darryl costume with jive talking
jokes.punchline: Offensive stereotypical impression
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Michael - inappropriate and offensive as intended

jokes.text: Monster Mash playing during costume show
jokes.punchline: Obvious song choice for Halloween
jokes.explanation: Generic Halloween atmosphere music - low effort

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: For Halloween, I'm playing 'Thriller' by Michael Jackson.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Dwight, that's the most obvious Halloween song choice ever.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's a classic. And I've perfected the zombie walk.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Yeah, we can tell.

jokes.text: Angela as sexy nurse costume reluctantly
jokes.setup: Costume contest with Angela's usual conservatism
jokes.punchline: Angela forced into revealing costume
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - Angela's discomfort is the joke

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: I am not wearing this. It's completely inappropriate and far too revealing.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Angela, you're a cat. It's a cat costume.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: The neckline is indecent. I look like a common street walker.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Angela, it's literally just ears and whiskers.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Well, I won't do it. I'll forfeit the contest.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Angela, the prize is a $50 Visa gift card.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Oh. Where's my tail?

jokes.text: Ouija board spelling A-S-S-E-T for Darryl
jokes.punchline: Spelling seems to go toward 'ass' but becomes 'asset'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that builds tension then resolves positively

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Is Darryl an asset to this office?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Okay, everyone put your fingers on.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's moving...

dialogue.speaker: Kelly
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait... A-S-S-E-T. Asset!

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: The spirits have spoken.

jokes.text: Halpert, you looking for someone to bang your wife?
jokes.setup: Jim questioning Danny about not calling Pam back
jokes.punchline: Danny's crude interpretation of Jim's motives
jokes.explanation: Shocking line that cuts through the politeness

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Why didn't you call Pam back?

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: Oh, I see what this is. You want me to call her back so you can... you know.

jokes.text: Erin bobbing for apples and eating them
jokes.punchline: Erin's enthusiastic participation and consumption
jokes.explanation: Fits Erin's character but not particularly funny

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: I love Halloween! I'm going to bob for apples, and candy corn, and those little marshmallow things!

jokes.text: Danny calling Pam dorky as reason for not calling back
jokes.setup: Everyone wanting to know why Danny didn't call
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Harsh truth that creates genuine discomfort - effective cringe

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: I didn't call because I didn't want to.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: That's... that's it?

dialogue.speaker: Danny
dialogue.line: That's it. Brutal honesty.

jokes.text: Darryl listing what everyone else did for him vs Michael
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he did things for Darryl
jokes.punchline: Systematic dismantling of Michael's contributions
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation that builds to devastating conclusion about Michael's irrelevance

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I've done so much for you, Darryl. I've mentored you, I've supported you, I've believed in you when nobody else did.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Michael, you hired me. That's literally your job.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But I did it with heart.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: You also sexually harassed me multiple times and I had to transfer to the warehouse to get away from you.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That was just me being friendly.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: You asked me if my pants were tight because of my 'big legs' or 'other reasons.'

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I was complimenting your physique!

jokes.text: Michael's hat solution for decision making
jokes.setup: Trying to resolve office hierarchy issues
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical hat-based solution
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael illogical solution but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, I've got it. We need to establish a clear chain of command.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: I agree. Perhaps a memo outlining—

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no. Hats. Different colored hats for different levels.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Red hat means you're in charge. Blue hat means you take orders. Yellow hat means... you're a hat.

dialogue.speaker: Gabe
dialogue.line: That doesn't make any sense.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Exactly. That's why it'll work. Nobody will see it coming.

jokes.text: Andy's Cornell a cappella story interrupting Darryl's problems
jokes.punchline: Andy making it about his college experience
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy self-absorption but could be stronger

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: I'm worried about my job security here. The company's been cutting costs.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh man, that reminds me of when I was at Cornell. We had budget cuts in the fraternity too. But you know what? We just worked harder, networked better, and I ended up becoming Vice President of the house. You just gotta have the right attitude and maybe go to an Ivy League school.

jokes.text: Who doesn't call a dork like that back?
jokes.punchline: Jim's protective response using same language
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Jim's loyalty while embracing the 'insult'

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Hey, don't call her dorky. She's not dorky. I'm dorky. She's... she's cool.

jokes.text: Jim and Pam as Popeye and Olive Oyl doing voices
jokes.punchline: Both doing character voices
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier setup but not particularly strong comedy

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I'm Detective Jim Halpert, and I'm here to solve the mystery of the missing donuts.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Well Detective, I'm Officer Pam Beesly, and I think I know who the culprit is.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Who could it be, Officer Beesly?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I'm looking right at him. You ate them all, didn't you?

jokes.text: Oscar winning costume contest despite rational consumer concept
jokes.setup: Oscar criticizing the contest and prize
jokes.punchline: Him winning the thing he was mocking
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic conclusion to Oscar's arc

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: This contest is ridiculous. The prize is probably something worthless anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And the winner is... Oscar!

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: What did I win?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: A Dundie Award!

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: ...I hate my life.

tmdb_overview: When Darryl takes an idea to corporate over his head, Michael freaks out. Everyone in the office is in a competitive mood as the Halloween costume contest gets underway. Meanwhile, Pam tries to get the truth out of Danny about their dating history.
insight: Costume Contest leans hard on cringe but underdelivers: 48 jokes yield just 64-point score.
